---
# generated by https://github.com/hashicorp/terraform-plugin-docs
page_title: "usageregistry Provider"
description: |-
  Provider for recording resource usage annotations in DynamoDB.
---

# usageregistry Provider

The usageregistry provider records usage relationships in DynamoDB. It does not read or write the resources being referenced, such as Vault secrets. It only records that a consumer, such as a repository, uses a target, such as a secret path.

The DynamoDB table must have a string partition key named `pk` and a string sort key named `sk`. To look up targets by consumer, add a GSI with partition key `sk` and sort key `pk`.

## DynamoDB Table

The DynamoDB table can be managed with Terraform. Use a static value or local value for the table name, then pass the same value to the usageregistry provider. Add `depends_on` to registry resources that are created in the same Terraform configuration as the table.

```terraform
terraform {
  required_providers {
    aws = {
      source  = "hashicorp/aws"
      version = "~> 6.0"
    }

    usageregistry = {
      source = "projectbro/usageregistry"
    }
  }
}

variable "region" {
  type    = string
  default = "ap-northeast-2"
}

variable "profile" {
  type    = string
  default = "platform-prod"
}

locals {
  usage_registry_table_name = "resource-usage-registry"
}

provider "aws" {
  region  = var.region
  profile = var.profile
}

resource "aws_dynamodb_table" "usage_registry" {
  name         = local.usage_registry_table_name
  billing_mode = "PAY_PER_REQUEST"
  hash_key     = "pk"
  range_key    = "sk"

  attribute {
    name = "pk"
    type = "S"
  }

  attribute {
    name = "sk"
    type = "S"
  }

  global_secondary_index {
    name            = "consumer-target-index"
    hash_key        = "sk"
    range_key       = "pk"
    projection_type = "ALL"
  }
}
```

The primary key stores usage records:

- `pk`: `target#<target.type>#<target.id>`
- `sk`: `consumer#<consumer.type>#<consumer.id>`

The same table also stores registry type items:

- target types: `pk = registry#target_type`, `sk = <target_type_name>`
- consumer types: `pk = registry#consumer_type`, `sk = <consumer_type_name>`

The plural type data sources query those exact partition keys. They do not scan the table.

The `consumer-target-index` GSI enables reverse lookup from a consumer to the targets it uses by querying `sk`.

## Provider Usage

```terraform
provider "usageregistry" {
  dynamodb_table = local.usage_registry_table_name
  region         = var.region
  profile        = var.profile
}

resource "usageregistry_target_type" "vault_secret" {
  depends_on = [aws_dynamodb_table.usage_registry]

  name    = "vault_secret"
  actions = ["read"]
}

resource "usageregistry_consumer_type" "repository" {
  depends_on = [aws_dynamodb_table.usage_registry]

  name = "repository"
}

resource "usageregistry_record" "vault_secret" {
  depends_on = [
    usageregistry_target_type.vault_secret,
    usageregistry_consumer_type.repository,
  ]

  target {
    type    = "vault_secret"
    id      = "kv/secret-source/common/db"
    action  = "read"
    version = "latest"
  }

  consumer {
    type = "repository"
    id   = "https://git.projectbro.com/Devops/example-service"
  }

  annotations = {
    ownership = "platform"
    workspace = terraform.workspace
  }
}
```

`usageregistry_record` validates `target.type`, `target.action`, and `consumer.type` against registered type items during create and update. Delete does not validate the type registry.

You can read one registered type or list all registered types:

```terraform
data "usageregistry_target_type" "vault_secret" {
  name = "vault_secret"
}

data "usageregistry_target_types" "all" {}

data "usageregistry_consumer_type" "repository" {
  name = "repository"
}

data "usageregistry_consumer_types" "all" {}
```

You can also configure explicit static credentials:

```terraform
provider "usageregistry" {
  dynamodb_table    = local.usage_registry_table_name
  region            = var.region
  access_key_id     = var.access_key_id
  secret_access_key = var.secret_access_key
  session_token     = var.session_token
}
```

If neither `profile` nor `access_key_id`/`secret_access_key` are configured, the provider falls back to the AWS SDK default credential chain.

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `access_key_id` (String, Sensitive) AWS access key ID to use explicitly. Must be set with `secret_access_key`.
- `dynamodb_endpoint` (String) Optional DynamoDB endpoint override, useful for DynamoDB Local.
- `dynamodb_table` (String) DynamoDB table used to store usage records. Can also be set with `USAGEREGISTRY_DYNAMODB_TABLE`.
- `profile` (String) AWS shared configuration profile to use explicitly. Conflicts with `access_key_id`, `secret_access_key`, and `session_token`.
- `region` (String) AWS region for DynamoDB. If omitted, the AWS SDK default region resolution is used.
- `secret_access_key` (String, Sensitive) AWS secret access key to use explicitly. Must be set with `access_key_id`.
- `session_token` (String, Sensitive) Optional AWS session token to use with explicit access key credentials.
